memory_centric_hypothesis: Memory-centric benefit is limited unless shared prefixes or high concurrency make cache placement important.
falsification_signal: If latency and cost are predicted by model size, prompt length, output length, and batching with negligible cache reuse benefit.

session_length_driver: number of turns and retained history
dominant_memory_objects: weights; KV cache; prefix cache; trajectory log
memory_centric_hypothesis: History placement, summarization, and KV retention affect latency and context quality as sessions grow.
falsification_signal: If summarizing or evicting prior turns has no measurable latency, cost, or quality effect beyond token count.

session_length_driver: documents per batch and document length
memory_centric_hypothesis: Memory-centric policy should have limited benefit beyond keeping weights resident and fitting transient KV.
falsification_signal: If independent-item throughput is dominated by arithmetic/batching and memory-object policies do not change throughput or cost.

session_length_driver: query volume, retrieved chunks, and corpus version cadence
branching_driver: retrieval fanout with low speculative branch
dominant_memory_objects: weights; KV cache; prefix cache; retrieved context; semantic cache entry
memory_centric_hypothesis: Placement of popular chunks, exact prefixes, and semantic results can reduce repeated retrieval and prefill work.
falsification_signal: If retrieval chunk reuse is flat, stale risk dominates, and cache hits do not reduce end-to-end latency or cost.

session_length_driver: edit-test iterations, file tree size, and context carryover
dominant_memory_objects: weights; KV cache; prefix cache; tool output; branch state; verifier state; trajectory log; durable workspace
memory_centric_hypothesis: Tool outputs, workspace state, and verifier traces become correctness-sensitive memory objects with longer lifetimes than KV.
falsification_signal: If all useful state can be reconstructed cheaply from the current prompt and repository without retaining tool or trajectory artifacts.

session_length_driver: research cycles, source count, and report length
branching_driver: hypotheses, source choices, and analysis branches
dominant_memory_objects: weights; KV cache; retrieved context; tool output; trajectory log; durable workspace; semantic cache entry
memory_centric_hypothesis: Durable notes, source excerpts, computed outputs, and provenance need tiering and compression separate from decode-time KV.
falsification_signal: If discarding tool outputs and notes never increases rerun cost, audit failure, or final-report error rate.

session_length_driver: candidate count and validation loop count
branching_driver: high fanout across candidates and counterexamples
tool_use_driver: tests, theorem tools, static analyzers, audits
dominant_memory_objects: weights; KV cache; branch state; verifier state; tool output; trajectory log
memory_centric_hypothesis: Verifier state and counterexamples drive future branch selection, so eviction risk is correctness-sensitive.
falsification_signal: If verifier traces can be discarded after each candidate without duplicate work, false acceptance, or missed regression.

session_length_driver: number of agents, branch depth, and merge cadence
dominant_memory_objects: weights; KV cache; prefix cache; branch state; verifier state; trajectory log; durable workspace
memory_centric_hypothesis: Trajectory-level scheduling and branch-aware residency outperform request-level policies when future branch reuse is uncertain.
falsification_signal: If branch-local state is never reused after merge and merge quality is unchanged by evicting branch evidence.

session_length_driver: dataset size and output length distribution
memory_centric_hypothesis: Memory-centric placement mostly affects weight residency and transient KV capacity, not durable agent state.
falsification_signal: If optimized arithmetic throughput and batching explain cost/latency while cache residency and object lifetimes add no predictive value.
